methodsAttempted.query: site:gu.se Göteborgs universitet generativ AI policy ChatGPT examination students
methodsAttempted.notes: Official GU and Student Portal results exposed Swedish student guidance, PIL examination guidance, and official study-rules PDFs.
Swedish-language discovery found the canonical student guidance and cheating during examination pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:pil.gu.se University of Gothenburg generative AI Copilot ChatGPT Edu personal data
methodsAttempted.notes: Official PIL pages supplied teacher-facing guidance and AI-service availability notes; one legal-and-ethical subpage returned 403 and was not used as evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:studentportal.gu.se "Rules for Studies at First- and Second-Cycle Level" "generative AI"
methodsAttempted.notes: The official English policy-document PDF was fetched directly and converted to normalized text outside the repository.

methodsAttempted.query: Firecrawl search for official University of Gothenburg AI policy sources
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned HTTP 402 before yielding results, so discovery proceeded with public search and direct official HTTP fetches.

artifacts.summary: Official University of Gothenburg discovery found Swedish student guidance, Swedish academic-integrity guidance, an official study-rules PDF, and PIL AI-in-higher-education pages. Login-gated SharePoint guidance, a 403 legal-and-ethical page, a news article, and a department/course-specific IBT policy were rejected as evidence for university-wide public claims.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Gothenburg study-rules PDF states that, for take-home examinations, written tasks, or equivalent, AI-generated content may not be presented as one’s own work and examiners should specify the limits for permitted use of generative AI tools.

artifacts.claimText: University of Gothenburg student guidance on cheating says generative AI use counts as cheating when it is used to complete an examination task the student is expected to do independently, to present AI-generated material as the student’s own work, or in other ways prohibited in the course or programme.
artifacts.normalizedValue: unauthorized generative AI use can count as cheating

artifacts.sourceTitle: Fusk vid examination
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Generativ AI kan skapa nytt innehåll. Universitetet förbjuder inte användning av generativ AI, men det räknas som fusk om du använder det för att: Genomföra en examinationsuppgift som du som student förväntas göra själv; Presenterar AI-genererad text eller annat material som om det vore ditt eget arbete; Även andra sätt att använda generativ AI kan vara otillåtna i din kurs eller i ditt program.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI-generated material in higher education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: At present, all staff and students have access to the chat service Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat. In addition, employees have access to ChatGPT Edu. artifacts.title: University of Gothenburg AI Policy Staged Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official University of Gothenburg sources support staged candidate claims that generative AI is not prohibited university-wide in education, that teachers and examiners decide and communicate course or examination-level rules, that AI detection tools are not recommended, that AI-generated content may not be presented as one’s own work, that unauthorized generative AI use may count as cheating, and that PIL guidance describes Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat, ChatGPT Edu, and personal-data cautions.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Firecrawl search returned HTTP 402, so source discovery used public search and direct official HTTP fetches.
A PIL legal-and-ethical aspects page returned HTTP 403 and was not used as evidence.
SharePoint Staff Portal guidance linked from PIL was treated as login-gated and was not fetched or used as evidence.
The IBT AI policy PDF was rejected because it appears department/course-specific and was not used for university-wide claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
